title: Part Enabler, Part Buffer: The Bind of the Justice Dept.’s No. 2
summary: Todd Blanche, the deputy attorney general, has helped usher in President Trump’s retribution campaign. But he faces anger on the right for resisting some of the most extreme measures.

title: Lawsuit Challenges Warrantless Searches and Forced Entries by ICE
summary: A coalition of legal groups claims the Homeland Security Department adopted an unconstitutional policy allowing its agents to enter homes without a judicial warrant.

title: How Trump Boxed Himself In on Iran
summary: President Trump faces the possibility that at the end of his own two-to-three week window for wrapping up the war in Iran, nothing much will have changed.

title: War With Iran Clarifies Trump’s Spending Priorities: The Military, Not Child Care
summary: As the White House prepares to release its 2027 budget, President Trump said military protection, not social programs, took precedence.

title: In the Birthright Citizenship Hearing, a Story of Asians Fighting for Rights
summary: Supreme Court justices and lawyers cited a litany of historic cases that reflect the many times Asians turned to the courts, trying to shape immigration law.

title: In Supreme Court Birthright Citizenship Case, Trump’s Likely Loss May Not End the Fight
summary: The justices gave respectful consideration to what was once a fringe theory and could rule against it on grounds that would allow Congress to return to the question.

title: A President, the Supreme Court and a Landmark Citizenship Order Collide
summary: The justices seems poised to rule against the president’s birthright citizenship plan. He is already furious over their decision rejecting his tariffs program.

title: Trump’s Media-Bashing Is Coming Back to Bite Him in Court
summary: Judges have cited attacks on the press by the president and his appointees when ruling against the government in at least three court cases.

title: Tina Peters, Colorado Election Denier, Will Have Prison Sentence Reconsidered
summary: Ms. Peters, a former county clerk, received a nine-year sentence after being convicted of tampering with voting machines. An appeals court overturned the sentence but did not immediately free her from prison.

title: What Drives Patrick Radden Keefe?
summary: Patrick Radden Keefe’s carefully applied ambition has propelled him to a rarefied perch.

title: Investigators Said to Look at Whether LaGuardia Controller Stepped Away
summary: Officials are said to be examining whether a controller had to use an emergency phone just before the crash at LaGuardia. They have yet to determine the cause of the crash and are also focusing on other avenues.

title: Why People With Chronic Illness Are Turning to AI Chatbots for Health Advice
summary: Some women with complex chronic illnesses are using chatbots to search for diagnoses or relief from their symptoms.

title: How A.I. Helped One Man (and His Brother) Build a $1.8 Billion Company
summary: Who needs more than two employees when artificial intelligence can do so many corporate tasks? It’s super efficient — and a little bit lonely.

title: Kanye West Plays First American Concert Since 2021
summary: After years of antisemitic behavior, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West performed his first full live show in the United States since 2021.

title: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson on Marriage, Secrets and ‘The Drama’
summary: The stars of the new film, “The Drama,” debate the pros and cons of dredging up the past.

title: ‘The Drama’ Review: Her Secret Is His Crisis
summary: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson play a couple upended when one confesses to coming close to committing a horrific crime.

title: Every President Tries It. It Never Works.
summary: What’s really behind all these attempts to revive American industry?

title: To Keep Child Abuse Off the Internet, He Has to Watch It
summary: Removing child sexual abuse material from the internet exacts a heavy toll on the workers tasked with reviewing it.

title: Primetime Trump
summary: We have takeaways from the president’s address to the nation.

title: Russia Is Sending a Second Oil Tanker to Fuel-Starved Cuba
summary: The announcement of a second shipment follows the arrival of a first oil tanker sent by the Kremlin earlier this week.

title: Artemis II Completes First Day of Its NASA Lunar Mission
summary: The crew, three Americans and a Canadian, are the first humans to travel to the moon in more than 50 years. They will not land on the surface, but the mission will pave the way for future visits.

title: Pope Leo Returns to Tradition, Washing Priests’ Feet
summary: Pope Francis washed and kissed the feet of prisoners and refugees. On Thursday, his successor performed the rite of humility and service for priests.

title: Ancient Artifacts Stolen in Dutch Museum Heist Are Recovered
summary: The golden helmet of Cotofenesti, a highly regarded artifact from Romania, and two elaborate golden bracelets were taken in January 2025.
